rulings.Case Title: JANINE BOCCIARDI VS. APPEARANCE ENHANCEMENT CLINIC, A MEDICAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 15, 2012, Line 7, DEFENDANT FRED SUESS, M.D. DBA APPEARANCE ENHANCEMENT CLINIC MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS Denied. There are triable issues whether: 1) Plaintiff consented, 2) Plaintiff was an appropriate candidate for the procedure, 3) the procedures were substantially different, 4) Defendant made false representations to Plaintiff, 5) Plaintiff relied on Defendant's representations, and 6) Defendant had a duty to disclose to Plaintiff his decision to change the procedure. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HK

rulings.Case Title: JAMES S. MADOW, INDIVIDUALLY AND ON BEHALF OF HIS VS. POST CONSTRUCTION SERVICES, L.P., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Court Proceedings Against Defts
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 15, 2012, Line 11, DEFENDANT SOUTH BAY REAL ESTATE COMMERCE GROUP, LLC'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Court Proceedings Against Defts Denied. Defendant has not shown that there is an enforceable arbitration agreement covering this case. The equitable estoppel argument is unavailing because the claims asserted in the Second Amended Complaint as a whole are not dependent on, or inextricably intertwined with, the Loanvest I, LLC Operating Agreement. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: TRIBECA COMPANIES, LLC VS. FIRST AMERICAN TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 15, 2012, Line 12, DEFENDANT FIRST AMERICAN TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is Overruled as to the fourth, fifth and sixth causes of action as to Tribeca and sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the seventh and eighth causes of action as to Tribecca and all causes of action as to Sky Pacific Holdings. The fourth, fifth and sixth claims are adequately pled as to Tribecca. As to the seventh and eighth causes of action, Tribeca is granted leave t oplead how the representation was made, by whom, and that the representer had authority to speak. As to the fourth, fifth and sixth causes of action, Sky Pacific Holdings is granted leave to allege facts showing it has standing to assert these causes of action against Defendant American Title. As to the seventh and eighth causes of action, Sky Pacific Holdings is granted leave to plead a misrepresentation made to it. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 15, 2012, Line 20, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS Sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the causes of action for assault, battery, sexual battery, false imprisonment, negligence, intentional, reckless or negligent infliction of severe emotional distress, and sustained without leave to amend as to the cause of action for unfair business practices. rulings.Case Title: REED TAYLOR VS. ROBERT MOONEY et al

Mr. Cato failed to properly serve the Summons and Complaint on defendant U.S. Security Associates Inc. rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 15, 2012, Line 27, DEFENDANTS DAN LEE AND PHOENIX RABBIT'S MOTION To Require That A Pltf Post A Bond is Granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk
